scenario.problem: It took 15 days to onboard a new developer because infrastructure access, repository creation, and CI/CD setup were manual processes owned by a single overloaded DevOps engineer. Consistency was poor, resulting in security vulnerabilities slipping into production.

implementation.solution: Built an Internal Developer Platform using Spotify's Backstage. Integrated Terraform templates.
implementation.golden_paths: Created two strict 'Golden Paths': One for Spring Boot microservices, one for React frontends. Both automatically injected Datadog tracking and SonarQube scans.

results.error_rate: Configuration-related incidents dropped by 50% due to standardized IaC generation.

lessons: Golden Paths don't just speed up development; they are the most effective way to organically enforce security policies across a growing engineering team.
Platform Engineering is a product management function. You must 'sell' the Golden Paths to the developers by making them the easiest route to production.
anti_patterns: Mandating the Platform. Good Golden Paths are adopted voluntarily because they save developers time. If you have to force them, they are too restrictive.
